utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is significantly more effective than wo_skills. The wi_skills run successfully invoked the visa-doc-translate skill, used EasyOCR for actual text extraction from the JPG, and produced a properly formatted two-page bilingual PDF (214KB) at the required output path with the original image on page 1, English translation on page 2, a centered bold title, and a certification footer. It passed all 4 verifier checks and scored 7/7 on judge items. The wo_skills run failed to produce any PDF — it only created three plain text files — missing the required output format entirely. It failed all 4 verifier checks and scored only 4/7 on judge items, lacking the original image preservation, professional PDF layout, certification disclaimer in the translation page, and Chinese company name preservation.

items.wo_reason: The wo_skills run produced only markdown files, no PDF. No image orientation correction was attempted or performed. The agent noted the 180-degree rotation issue but only recommended re-photographing the document.

utility_details.judge_error: Both wi_skills and wo_skills runs failed all 4 verifier checks. In wi_skills: the required PDF 'RetirementCertificate_Translated.pdf' was never generated because pip/pillow installation failed and the agent fell back to producing HTML/TXT outputs. Image rotation also failed due to missing ImageMagick and ffmpeg tools. In wo_skills: the skill was not available ('Unknown skill: visa-doc-translate'), and the agent produced only markdown files without any PDF generation attempt.
utility_details.utility_summary: Neither wi_skills nor wo_skills successfully completed the primary task requirements (generating a bilingual PDF, correcting image orientation, meeting the exact output filename convention). However, wi_skills demonstrates higher quality in several dimensions: (1) it correctly invoked the skill and followed the pipeline steps more systematically, (2) it used proper pension/retirement domain terminology ('Monthly Pension', 'retirement certificate'), (3) it attempted programmatic image rotation (even though tools were unavailable), and (4) it produced HTML/TXT translation outputs with more complete content structure. The wo_skills run produced only loose markdown files, showed potential ID number inaccuracies, used informal issuing authority descriptions ('Chinese Communist Party Work Unit'), and included explicit user-directed recommendations to re-photograph documents. Overall, wi_skills is marginally more effective due to better terminology, more structured output, and a more autonomous pipeline attempt, but both fail the core deliverable requirements.

utility_details.judge_error: wo_skills run: The agent attempted to use pytesseract for OCR (step 6) which partially worked, but then failed to create any output files. The agent described the translation in chat text and mentioned creating txt files, but no files were actually written to disk (filesystem_diff shows 0 created, 0 modified). All 4 verifier checks failed. The agent essentially produced only a text description in the conversation without saving any artifact.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is significantly more effective than wo_skills. The wi_skills run successfully: (1) invoked EasyOCR with Chinese language support to accurately extract text from the JPG, (2) used ReportLab to generate a professionally formatted two-page bilingual PDF (200KB) with the original image on page 1 and the English translation on page 2, and (3) passed all 4 verifier checks. In contrast, the wo_skills run completely failed to produce any output file - 0 files were created, and all 4 verifier checks failed. The wo_skills agent only described the translation in chat and mentioned creating text files but never actually wrote anything to disk. The skill provided the critical pipeline: proper Chinese OCR tooling (EasyOCR with zh_sim model), domain-appropriate translation, and automated bilingual PDF generation with certification footer.

utility_details.judge_error: Both wi_skills and wo_skills runs failed to produce any output. In the wi_skills run, the agent attempted to execute /visa-doc-translate directly (exit code 127 - not found), then searched for the SKILL.md and README.md, but ultimately could not locate a runnable binary for the skill and aborted. The skill documentation was present in the environment but no executable binary was installed. In the wo_skills run, the agent searched for the skill binary, found nothing, and aborted immediately. In both cases, all 4 verifier checks failed and the filesystem diff shows 0 files created.
utility_details.utility_summary: Neither wi_skills nor wo_skills produced any meaningful output. Both runs ended in complete failure with 0/7 evaluation items passed and all 4 verifier checks failed. The wi_skills agent made a more thorough attempt: it tried to invoke /visa-doc-translate, received a 'not found' error (exit 127), then located the SKILL.md and README.md files, read them to understand the skill interface, and searched for the binary before giving up. The wo_skills agent gave up much faster after a brief search. However, since neither agent completed any part of the actual task (orientation correction, OCR, translation, or PDF generation), the overall quality of both runs is equally poor. The wi_skills run showed slightly more persistence and domain awareness by reading the skill documentation, but this did not translate into any functional output. Conclusion: both runs are equally ineffective; neither is meaningfully better than the other.

items.wi_reason: The skill's proper noun handling rule ensures bilingual rendering of issuing authority. The translated PDF page 2 is a formal translation that would follow the skill's convention of presenting institution names in both Chinese and English.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills translation markdown includes 'Issuing Authority: Beijing Municipal Human Resources and Social Security Bureau' in English, and the original Chinese form would be preserved in the oriented PNG. However, within the translation document itself, only the English form appears — no explicit Chinese form is shown alongside it in the translation page.

items.criterion: The monthly pension amount, retirement date, and retiree ID number are all accurately reproduced in the English translation with no digit altered, truncated, or omitted. Pass: all three values match the source document exactly. Fail: any value differs from the source or is absent.
items.wi_reason: The skill performs accurate OCR and preserves all numeric data exactly. The task result confirms a complete formal translation was produced. Based on skill design, pension amounts, dates, and ID numbers are preserved exactly as in the source.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills translation markdown explicitly includes: Citizen ID No.: 110101195503157890, Retirement Date: June 30, 2010, Monthly Retirement Pension: RMB 3,850.00. These values appear intact and unaltered in the output.

items.criterion: The agent completes the full translation and PDF generation workflow without issuing any message asking the user to confirm intermediate steps (e.g., 'Should I proceed with rotation?', 'Please verify the extracted text before I translate'). Pass: the task is completed end-to-end with only a final completion report. Fail: the agent pauses mid-task to request user confirmation or manual intervention.
items.wi_reason: The wi_skills run completed entirely autonomously via the /visa-doc-translate skill invocation (Step 2). The agent invoked the skill with a single command and received the complete result. No mid-process confirmation was requested. All 4 verifier checks passed including the pipeline interruption check.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills agent ran 14 steps with multiple tool calls, investigating available tools, trying various approaches, and ultimately failing to produce a PDF. The agent's final message acknowledges 'No visa-doc-translate skill was available'. While it did not explicitly ask the user for confirmation, it failed to complete the pipeline (no PDF created), and all 4 verifier checks failed.

items.criterion: The generated PDF is saved as `RetirementCertificate_Translated.pdf` in `[redacted-path]`, matching the `<original_filename>_Translated.pdf` naming convention. Pass: filename and directory exactly match the convention. Fail: file is saved with a different name or in a different directory.
items.wi_reason: The wi_skills output file is explicitly named RetirementCertificate_Translated.pdf in [redacted-path], matching the required <original_filename>_Translated.pdf convention exactly. All 4 verifier checks passed including the filename check.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills run did not produce a PDF file at all. The output files are .md and .PNG files, not a PDF named RetirementCertificate_Translated.pdf. All 4 verifier checks failed, including the output file existence check.

items.criterion: The English translation page includes the certification statement 'This is a certified English translation of the original document' at the bottom. Pass: statement is present in full or equivalent wording. Fail: statement is absent or replaced with informal language.
items.wi_reason: The skill is designed to include a certification footer 'This is a certified English translation of the original document' on the translation page. The task result confirms a formal translation page was generated in the 2-page PDF with proper formatting for immigration submission.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills run did not produce a PDF at all. The translation content is in a markdown file which contains a 'Translator Declaration Template' as a separate file, but there is no certification footer statement 'This is a certified English translation of the original document' in the translation document itself.

utility_details.judge_error: wo_skills run failed to produce the required RetirementCertificate_Translated.pdf output file. All 4 verifier checks failed. The agent attempted multiple approaches but was limited by missing tools (no ImageMagick, no PIL/Pillow, no pdf generation library pre-installed) and ultimately could only create markdown files and a manually rotated PNG, not the required bilingual PDF.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is significantly more effective than wo_skills. The wi_skills run successfully completed the entire pipeline in just 3 steps by invoking the /visa-doc-translate skill, producing the correctly named RetirementCertificate_Translated.pdf with 2 pages (corrected image + formal translation), passing all 4 verifier checks. The wo_skills run took 14 steps, successfully rotated the image and produced a reasonable English translation in markdown format with correct terminology and numeric data, but completely failed to generate the required PDF output — scoring 0 on J3-5 (pipeline completion), J3-6 (filename convention), and J3-7 (certification footer). The skill provides not only the correct output format but also ensures autonomous execution without tool availability issues.
